title: Feature-Colocated Services on Web
context: Frontend HTTP services can be centralised (`core/services/`) or colocated with their feature (`modules/admin/{feature}/`). Centralised services accumulate into "god services" as features grow. Colocated services keep features self-contained — deleting a feature is one folder deletion.
decision: **Each feature owns its own HTTP service and models file**, colocated in the feature folder.
├── actions.enums.ts        # as const enum objects (if needed)

- Services are `@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })` for singleton lifecycle. Root-provided services are retained for the entire app session — acceptable because feature services are stateless HTTP wrappers with no retained state. If a feature service grows to hold significant state, consider route-level providers.
- Only truly cross-cutting services (auth, error handling, translation) live in `core/services/`
consequences: - Features are self-contained — no god service accumulation
- Vertical slice on frontend mirrors backend feature structure
- Easy to understand what a feature depends on by reading one folder
- CRUD boilerplate duplicated across feature services (getById, getList, save, delete)

**Mitigation:** The boilerplate is mechanical and consistent. If duplication becomes painful at scale, a thin `HttpEntityService<T>` base class can DRY it up without centralising feature-specific logic.
origin: Identity convention (ADR-0007), adopted by Actions for vertical slice consistency.
